content.problemStatement:
Chef Ada constructed a simple undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) as follows:


Start with a complete graph with KKK vertices and K‚ãÖ(K‚àí1)/2K \cdot (K-1)/2K‚ãÖ(K‚àí1)/2 edges.
Then, add N‚àíKN-KN‚àíK vertices one by one. After adding each vetex, find some clique of K‚àí1K-1K‚àí1 vertices in the graph and connect the new vertex to all the vertices of that clique.
Consider NNN boolean variables X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äã,X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äã and their negations ¬¨X1,¬¨X2,‚Ä¶,¬¨XN\neg X_1, \neg X_2, \ldots, \neg X_N¬¨X1‚Äã,¬¨X2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,¬¨XN‚Äã, corresponding to vertices of the graph. You are given the initial values of all variables.

A CNF boolean expression is defined as a conjunction of clauses, where each clause is a disjunction of two or more elements of the set of variables and their negations.

Using the graph GGG, Ada wrote a CNF boolean expression BBB with MMM clauses, where each clause corresponds to a clique in the graph with some (maybe none) of the variables (corresponding to vertices) negated. More formally, for each iii from 111 to MMM:


Ada chose some clique in the graph, consisting of LiL_iLi‚Äã vertices Z1,Z2,‚Ä¶,ZLiZ_1, Z_2, \ldots, Z_{L_i}Z1‚Äã,Z2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ZLi‚Äã‚Äã
then for each valid jjj, Ada chose a variable Pi,jP_{i,j}Pi,j‚Äã as either XZjX_{Z_j}XZj‚Äã‚Äã or ¬¨XZj\neg X_{Z_j}¬¨XZj‚Äã‚Äã
the iii-th clause in the expression BBB had LiL_iLi‚Äã terms: Pi,1‚à®Pi,2‚à®‚Ä¶‚à®Pi,LiP_{i,1} \lor P_{i,2} \lor \ldots \lor P_{i,L_i}Pi,1‚Äã‚à®Pi,2‚Äã‚à®‚Ä¶‚à®Pi,Li‚Äã‚Äã
Ada wants the expression to evaluate to "true". In order to achieve that, she may change the values of variables; for each valid iii, the cost of changing the value of XiX_iXi‚Äã (from "true" to "false" or vice versa) is CiC_iCi‚Äã. Find the minimum total cost needed to make the expression BBB evaluate to "true" or determine that it is impossible.
constraints: each character of SSS is either '0' or '1'
1‚â§Li‚â§N1 \le L_i \le N1‚â§Li‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (25 points, time limit 1 second):** K=3K = 3K=3
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
The second line contains a single binary string SSS with length NNN describing the initial values of the variables. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of SSS is '1' if XiX_iXi‚Äã is true or '0' if XiX_iXi‚Äã is false.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains an integer LiL_iLi‚Äã followed by a space and LiL_iLi‚Äã space-separated integers representing the variables Pi,1,‚Ä¶,Pi,LiP_{i,1}, \ldots, P_{i,L_i}Pi,1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Pi,Li‚Äã‚Äã. For each valid iii, the variable XiX_iXi‚Äã is represented by the integer iii and its negation is represented by ‚àíi-i‚àíi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum cost needed to make the boolean expression evaluate to "true", or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it is impossible.

Explanation:
Example case 1: The following figure depicts the graph used to generate the expression.

The boolean expression is B=(X1‚à®¬¨X2‚à®X4)‚àß(X2‚à®X3‚à®¬¨X5)‚àß(X1‚à®¬¨X3‚à®X2)B = (X_1 \lor \neg X_2 \lor X_4) \land (X_2 \lor X_3 \lor \neg X_5) \land (X_1 \lor \neg X_3 \lor X_2)B=(X1‚Äã‚à®¬¨X2‚Äã‚à®X4‚Äã)‚àß(X2‚Äã‚à®X3‚Äã‚à®¬¨X5‚Äã)‚àß(X1‚Äã‚à®¬¨X3‚Äã‚à®X2‚Äã).
One optimal solution is to change the values of X1X_1X1‚Äã and X3X_3X3‚Äã.
